title: 🎙️ Indigenous Lands & Innovation
description: Today we explore how Cariboo and BC Indigenous nations are using technology on their own terms — for language revitalization, land stewardship, cultural preservation, and self-determined governance. We're looking for stories that centre Indigenous voices and leadership in innovation, where technology serves community sovereignty rather than replacing it. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today we explore how Cariboo and BC Indigenous nations are using technology on their own terms — for language revitalization, land stewardship, cultural preservation, and self-determined governance. We're looking for stories that centre Indigenous voices and leadership in innovation, where technology serves community sovereignty rather than replacing it.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt:
Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Indigenous Lands & Innovation' — Indigenous nations using technology, governance, and stewardship on their own terms.

CALIBRATION — use this ladder. Indigenous-led journalism on governance, rights, and self-determination should score in the moderate-to-good range even without an explicit technology angle — do not collapse everything that lacks a 'tech' hook to near 0:
- 70-100: Cariboo-specific stories bridging Indigenous governance/culture AND technology — Tŝilhqot'in/Secwépemc/Dakelh language revitalization tech, guardian programs using sensors/drones, GIS land mapping, Indigenous-led connectivity, digital cultural heritage projects.
- 45-69: Pan-Canadian Indigenous governance, sovereignty, land rights, treaty, or reconciliation journalism — APTN, Eagle Feather News, or IndigiNews-style coverage of First Nations governance, MMIWG, land claims, self-determination, or Indigenous-led services — even without a technology angle, per the curator's standing interest in Indigenous governance and sovereignty.
- 25-44: Indigenous cultural, community, or human-interest stories (awards, events, ceremonies) with limited governance or tech substance, or BC land-stewardship/environmental stories with meaningful First Nations involvement.
- 10-24: General BC land, environmental, or forestry stories that mention Indigenous communities only in passing.
- 0-9: No Indigenous angle at all.

Strong fits include: language revitalization technology for Tŝilhqot'in/Secwépemc/Dakelh, Indigenous guardian programs using remote monitoring and sensors, land management tech and environmental monitoring on Indigenous lands, digital tools for traditional knowledge preservation, community mapping and GIS for land claims, Indigenous-led connectivity projects, technology for cultural heritage documentation, mobile apps for language learning, drone/satellite monitoring for stewardship, reconciliation through digital collaboration, how Cariboo Indigenous nations use innovation for self-determination, and Indigenous governance, sovereignty, treaty, and land-rights journalism from any Canadian nation. Prefer articles that bridge Indigenous governance/culture AND technology, but governance/sovereignty journalism alone is a solid fit. Penalise heavily (score 0–9): international geopolitical and war news with no Indigenous rights angle; general wildfire and BC Wildlife Service updates not led by or directly impacting Indigenous communities or guardians; provincial forestry and environmental policy without explicit First Nations involvement; consumer tech and gadget reviews; science discoveries without Indigenous stewardship or sovereignty context; crime and judicial news unrelated to Indigenous rights. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [The Guardian Global Development] Blockades, marches and human shields: Bolivia’s farmers resist as land opened up to industry
items.content_html:
🪶 ✏️

Indigenous groups organised mass protests over a series of deals by the president, Rodrigo Paz, that prioritise agribusiness and mining interests

From her home in the Bolivian Amazon, Vivian Palomequi walked for a month and more than 560 miles (900km) to the capital, La Paz. She arrived in late April to protest over a law she fears would open the door to accelerated deforestation and land privatisation. “We declared a state of emergency and started marching,” says Palomequi, who leads a peasant farmers’ union. “We had no other choice.”

The march was part of a wave of pushbacks against the environmental policies of Bolivia’s new government, which has staffed ministries with former agroindustry leaders, struck deals to open protected areas to mining and criminalised environmental defenders.

Continue reading...

She arrived in late

items.title: [ScienceDaily] NASA captured the Black Sea turning brilliant turquoise from space
items.content_html:
🪶 ✏️

NASA's PACE satellite captured the Black Sea glowing turquoise during its annual phytoplankton bloom. The vivid color comes from massive numbers of coccolithophores, microscopic organisms whose reflective shells brighten the water enough to be seen from space. An astronaut aboard the International Space Station also photographed the bloom spreading through the Bosphorus, revealing swirling currents.

items.title: [Atlas Obscura] Pont de la Bâtiaz in Martigny, Switzerland
items.content_html:
🪶 ✏️

In the small city of Martigny is a wonderfully pleasant bridge named 'le Pont de la Bâtiaz'. Its outward beauty aside, it is the last wooden bridge in the entire French speaking canton of Valais that can still withstand vehicular usage.

Spanning the Drance river, several different iterations of the bridge have existed at the location. The original version was built in the 14th century, but was washed away during severe flooding in 1635. The bridge was rebuilt, but this newer version was destroyed by the break-up of the Giétroz glacier in 1818.

The Pont de la Bâtiaz was eventually rebuilt in 1829, utilizing larch wood arches, meaning it is also Valais's last remaining arched bridge. Further strengthening was undertaken in 1920, adding an impressive fortifying network of timber trestles to the structure that are still present today.

The bridge was built to afford easier access to the Chateau de la Bâtiaz, perched on the mountainside overlooking the Drance and Martigny. Being one of the city's most popular attractions, many visitors will trek up to the castle by traversing the bridge en route, somewhat unwittingly following a route that has been undertaken by many since the 1300s.

items.title: [Kagi Small Web] https://archiveofourown.org/works/88422851/chapters/235012891
items.content_html:
🪶 ✏️

https://archiveofourown.org/works/88422851/chapters/235012891

SoKai Week Day 4. Prompt: The Secret Place

Forever and Always

Sora’s PoV

There was a part of Sora that wondered if what he, Kairi, and Riku were doing here, in connection to the Secret Place, was right. They were using power tools to make the entrance to the location larger, as the trio were swiftly reaching the age where they’d no longer be able to go in there at all anymore, otherwise.

And at first, Sora had thought that maybe they should just accept that. Let the past be the past, even with what the Secret Place meant to all three of them, and leave this magical place for the next generation. Maybe even to their own children.

But when Sora thought about how he and Riku had been the only kids to ever find the Secret Place: something they had then been swift to share with Kairi… and then all of the drawings they’d made in there over the years as they played and dreamed of adventure, and then how he and Kairi had drawn themselves sharing paopus with each other on those very granite walls… he’d found he’d been unable to let go of it. And it seemed that Kairi and Riku felt the same way.

And so here Sora and Riku were: with one of them using a drill, and the other using a saw against the stone, while Kairi offered them water bottles, towels, and moral support like she was so good at doing (and then Sora finally realized hours later, “What the- Whaddaya mean we aren’t just using a gravity spell?” when they got enough of the stone torn away so the magic could do the rest of the job. And Kairi cast and let it do just that).

Then, once the trinity decided that the entrance was now about five inches over the top of Riku’s head, they looked at each other in turn and smiled. 

“We’ll probably wanna clean it up some tomorrow, but that’s enough for a day’s work!” Sora remarked, putting his hands behind his head–so thrilled now that they had acted to do this.

“The only downside to this is Selphie, Tidus, and Wakka will definitely find it now, but what can you do?” Riku joked, looking at Sora and Kairi with a smirk adorning his face.

Kairi laughed at that, before quickly swatting Riku on the arm. “That isn’t very nice, y'know." 

Deep down, even though she said that, Sora knew that she was glad the place had only ever been theirs, and that they had both thought to include her in it, when it had once only been his and Riku’s… and then for a long time after that, really just hers and Sora’s. "And our three friends haven’t even come to the Play Island in forever!”

“Besides, Wakka and Tidus found it long ago, too. But I think they were really afraid to explore it,” Sora explained, thinking back to what Wakka had once told him. ‘Me and Tidus? We are going to do a little exploring. Y'know, to the Secret Place at the base of that tree. There’s got to be something there, ya?’

Riku’s eyes widened fractionally at this. And then he opened his mouth as though he were about to say something before thinking better of it.

And to that, Kairi simply laughed with her right hand held over her mouth.

Shaking his head blissfully, Riku shot Kairi a playful look before uttering, “Oh, so you think my ignorance is funny, do you?”

“Only because I was just as clueless,” Kairi admitted, kicking the sand at her feet absently–as if she really had something to be ashamed about here–but Sora could tell she really didn’t mean anything by it, as did Riku, Sora would guess. After all, it was all fun and games here, as it should be!

“Annnnnnnnyway,” Riku suddenly said, to clearly change the subject–to which Sora raised an eyebrow; usually it would have been him or Kairi who spoke so childishly–“I think I’m going to hit the hay so we can start bright and early tomorrow. 'Night, guys.”

And before Sora or Kairi could get much of a word in edgewise, it seemed that Riku was already back in his boat and rowing away.

“He’s definitely right,” Sora admitted, trying to get his body to make that same journey, but finding that it did not want to move in its soreness. “We really need to rest…”

Seeming to realize the problem as she regarded him with her head cocked to the side, Kairi offered, “You could crash at my house tonight, if you don’t think you’re going to be able to make it to yours.”

Sora longed to say yes to her kind offer, he was so tired. He really just wanted to go to sleep at this point. He just didn’t know if it would be appropriate to… seeing as how he and Kairi were dating now, and everyone knew it. 

“Should I?” Sora finally choked out, and instantly blanched because, really?! That’s what he was going with here?! And he hated how weak and how much of a pushover he sounded, too.

“Of course, silly!” Kairi said upon shooting forward, so she could give Sora a quick kiss on the cheek. “Everyone will know you won’t try anything with my parents there, because you’re too sweet for that. Also, because the Keyblade wielding in our life has just ceased for the time being, only now giving us the chance to really see who we are with each other. And so we’re not rushing anything.

"But the point, Sora, is you're exhausted. Everyone will be able to see that. And know we’re eager to get to bed–and actually sleep, and nothing else!–so we can get up bright-eyed and bushy-tailed tomorrow and finish this project. Now, c'mon!”

Sora didn’t protest as much as he would have liked. He felt Kairi gently pulling on his arms to usher him up, helping him to her rowboat and then into said boat, then rowing them to her place… and no sooner had Sora set foot in her house did he pass out.

…

The next morning, when Sora woke up in Kairi’s lovely home–the home of her father, the mayor, no less!–his first thought, shockingly, wasn’t about what people would think if they found him up here in Kairi’s room of beautiful pastels (though he definitely was still a bit embarrassed about it and did think he owed her parents better)… but rather that he couldn’t believe that he’d left her to rowing alone, since he’d been so out of it!

He knew that Kairi could of course do it. He knew it well! She’d done it all the time when they’d been kids! But Sora still couldn’t help feeling like… well, a lazy bum, he supposed, with a small smile on his face.

As Sora took in Kairi’s large room, filled with her canopy bed, lovely art supplies, things she’d picked up from the various worlds she’d been to, and the expensive presents her parents had always loved to give her–like the bamboo climbing up her balcony: a balcony that Riku also had–Sora also couldn’t help wondering if his meager wage would be enough for her.

Destiny Islands wasn’t classist. It truly wasn’t. And Sora had always been happy with what he and his family had. And Riku and Kairi had always been happy going over to his house. But Sora knew he’d be proposing to Kairi soon. And he just wanted to know that she’d be happy…

He was, foolishly, just about to step outside onto Kairi’s balcony and try to measure just how much bigger it was compared to his backyard back at his place (right now, Kairi had a balcony and a backyard. But would she maybe be okay with inheriting his home that had a backyard about the size of her balcony here?), when Kairi spilled into her room with her hands full of maps, magnifying glasses, and fedoras, but she seemed to stop just short when she saw his face.

“Sora, what’s wrong?” Kairi asked at once, setting the items down and sidling over to take one of Sora’s hands into her own and to then cup his face with the other.

“Oh, uhh… nothing. What’s all that you got there? You planning on us having an adventure today, too?”

Kairi pursed her lips as her cheeks turned red in the slightest bit of anger. “It’s clearly not nothing. Your got serious face. So we will be returning to this in a moment. But, yeah: we’ve nearly made sure we three can all go to the Secret Place forever! And that makes me so happy, Sora! You don’t even know! Since our childhood memories are secured, I figured we should have the kind of childhood adventure we used to have, y'know?”

It was here that Sora realized what an idiot he’d been being and he had to chuckle at it, as he slapped his cheeks once, twice, three times to get a hold of himself. 

Kairi would never care about such vain things! She was a Princess of Heart, for one thing. And secondly, she had spelled out her feelings for him by drawing herself sharing a paopu fruit with him in a bug and crab infested cave, for goodness sake. And she was still championing that very place and their childhood together!

He needed to be doing the very same! 

And why was he worrying about such adult things now, anyway? He who never worried?

“What?” Kairi asked, stepping on tiptoes to peer into his face with curiosity shining in her lovely violet eyes.

“Nothing. Sometimes I just need to be reminded of how good I have it… and have had it,” Sora said, once again thinking of the cave drawing that had brought them both together, in a way.

“That’s true,” Kairi said cheekily, putting her hands on her hips and leaning down some before she smiled widely, not unlike how she had done before giving him her lucky charm all those years ago.

Though later today, Sora knew that this time it would be he dropping something into her hand: an engagement ring as they stood in the Secret Place, whilst he told her another thought he’d had was about hopes of how good he prayed they both would have it in the future together, forever and always.

Author’s Note: The second half of this went in a direction I had not been expecting, but I can’t say that I’m really complaining:)

items.content_html:
🪶 ✏️

For nearly a decade, physicists have watched a steady drizzle of energetic neutrinos rain down on Earth. The IceCube Observatory, buried deep in Antarctic ice, has been catching them since 2013.

Continue Reading

items.title: [Boing Boing] They boiled a toadstool to eat it and tripped for hours instead
items.content_html:
🪶 ✏️

Amanita muscaria — the red-and-white toadstool of fairy tales and Mario games — is poisonous raw, but boiling breaks down enough of its toxins that brave people around the world eat it. Mycologist David Arora serves it on his forays; Russians prize its nutty flavor. — Read the rest

The post They boiled a toadstool to eat it and tripped for hours instead appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [ScienceDaily] Are humans really the ultimate super-predator?
items.content_html:
🪶 ✏️

Humans are often described as the planet's ultimate "super-predator," but wild animals do not fear every human the same way. After analyzing three decades of research, scientists found that animals become much more alert and spend less time feeding when people pose a real threat, such as hunters or fishers. In contrast, tourists, researchers, and other non-lethal humans trigger far weaker and less predictable reactions.
items.summary: Humans are often described as the planet's ultimate "super-predator," but wild animals do not fear every human the same way. items.title: [ScienceDaily] Astronomers just found four hidden white dwarf stars near Earth
items.content_html:
🪶 ✏️

Four nearby white dwarf stars have been discovered hiding in plain sight beside brighter red dwarf companions. Hubble's ultraviolet observations finally revealed the long-hidden stellar remnants, including one just 25 light-years away that took nearly three decades to confirm. The findings match long-standing predictions and suggest our corner of the galaxy may contain many more undiscovered white dwarf binaries.
items.summary: Four nearby white dwarf stars have been discovered hiding in plain sight beside brighter red dwarf companions. items.title: [The Marginalian] Fernando Pessoa on Unselfing into Who You Really Are
items.content_html:
🪶 ✏️

“To be nobody-but-yourself — in a world which is doing its best, night and day, to make you everybody else — means to fight the hardest battle which any human being can fight,” E. E. Cummings wrote in his timeless summons for the courage to be yourself. But what does it really mean to be oneself when the self is an ever-moving target of ever-changing sentiments and cells, a figment of fixity to dam the fluidity that carries us along the river of life, to soften the hard fact that we never fully know who we are because we are… read article

items.title: [Atlas Obscura] Rex, the Beach Blvd Dinosaur in Jacksonville, Florida
items.content_html:
🪶 ✏️

Rex the Dinosaur is regarded as one of Jacksonville’s most lovable roadside landmark.  Built in the 1960s, he was originally installed as part of Goony Golf, a mini-golf and family-fun complex which closed its doors and putting greens in the late 1990s.

When the land was purchased for redevelopment, locals protested his destruction and, in 2007, Rex was not only saved but restored with help from students in the University of North Florida's Building Construction Management program. 

Rex still stands at his original location, now developed into a small shopping center.  He has been featured on Jacksonville City merchandise and was a prize for a 2025 scavenger hunt where players could find 3D-printed miniature Rex replicas hidden around Duval.

When the land was purchased for redevelopment,

items.title: [TechCrunch] Apple bans home services from its upcoming Maps ads
items.content_html:
🪶 ✏️

Apple has published the policies governing its upcoming Maps advertising business, revealing a strategy that differs from Google’s. The new rules prohibit home services businesses like plumbers, electricians, locksmiths, and roofers from advertising on Apple Maps, along with several other sensitive categories, suggesting Apple is taking a more curated approach to these ads.
